compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
RISK 1 (cross-session duplication). Two independently-authored modules each define their OWN `RetryExhaustedError` with a DIFFERENT base class, each raised by a separate retry loop and re-exported from a different public surface. A caller writing `except RetryExhaustedError` against one import silently MISSES the one raised by the other path ‚Äî exception identity drives control flow, so this is a latent reliability bug, not cosmetic.

## Why
Verified ground truth: `acli_subprocess.py:124` ‚Üí `class RetryExhaustedError(RuntimeError)` (re-exported via `acli.py`); `batch_dispatch.py:39` ‚Üí `class RetryExhaustedError(Exception)` (re-exported via `applier.py`). So `acli.RetryExhaustedError is not applier.RetryExhaustedError`. `JiraAPIError` has ONE body (`batch_dispatch.py:31`) re-exported through both surfaces ‚Äî only the re-export, not the class, is duplicated. The `acli` raise site chains (`raise ... from last_error`); the `batch_dispatch` site does NOT (`raise RetryExhaustedError(str(last_exc))` ‚Äî drops the cause).

## What ‚Äî OSS-informed design (requests/urllib3/httpx/tenacity + PEP 3134)
Consensus: one package-level base in a dedicated errors module; subclass `Exception` (or the single most-derived stdlib type a caller already catches); carry the last failure; chain the cause. `_errors.py` already exists (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_errors.py`, ~67 LOC) ‚Äî ADD the unified types there:
- `class ReconcilerError(Exception)` ‚Äî the catch-all base seam (per requests `RequestException` / urllib3 `HTTPError`).
- `class RetryExhaustedError(ReconcilerError, RuntimeError)` ‚Äî MRO `RetryExhaustedError‚ÜíReconcilerError‚ÜíRuntimeError‚ÜíException` is consistent and is caught by BOTH existing `except RuntimeError` (acli path) AND `except Exception` (batch path) sites, so NO call-site breaks. `__init__(self, message="", *, last_exception=None, attempts=None)` ‚Äî message-first keeps both current `RetryExhaustedError("‚Ä¶")` call sites working; the optional fields mirror tenacity `RetryError.last_attempt` / urllib3 `MaxRetryError.reason`. Do NOT list `(RuntimeError, Exception)` literally (redundant/duplicate base ‚Äî pylint E0241).
- `class JiraAPIError(ReconcilerError)` ‚Äî move the single body from `batch_dispatch.py:31` here, keeping `__init__(self, message, status_code)` verbatim.
Then delete both `RetryExhaustedError` bodies and `import` the canonical one (`from rebar_reconciler._errors import RetryExhaustedError`); leave the `acli.py`/`applier.py` `__all__` re-exports (they now re-export the SAME object). Fix the chaining gap at `batch_dispatch.py:137`: `raise RetryExhaustedError("‚Ä¶", last_exception=last_exc, attempts=max_retries+1) from last_exc`.

## Scope
In: unify the two exception types in `_errors.py`, repoint both modules, fix the missing `from` chain. FOLLOW-ON (separate, not this ticket): consolidate the 5 HTTP/subprocess retry LOOPS (push/batch_dispatch/acli_subprocess√ó2/acli_rest) onto one `retry(fn, *, attempts, backoff, retry_on)` helper ‚Äî linked as discovered_from.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.acli import RetryExhaustedError` and `from ...applier import RetryExhaustedError` resolve to the SAME object (`is` identity test).
- [ ] A `RetryExhaustedError` raised by either retry loop is caught by `except RuntimeError`, `except Exception`, AND `except ReconcilerError`.
- [ ] Both raise sites chain the cause (`__cause__` is the last underlying exception); `last_exception`/`attempts` populated.
- [ ] `JiraAPIError` defined once in `_errors.py`, `__init__(message, status_code)` unchanged; both surfaces import it.
- [ ] Tests cover the cross-path `except` identity + chaining; existing reconciler tests pass.

file_impact.reason: remove dup classes + fix raise-from chaining

compiled_state.title: Risk 1: unify reconciler exception taxonomy + chaining (OSS-informed)
